structural_note: Both are the main hub of their network. Lausanne slightly higher raw count due to tram/metro but structurally equivalent as convergence point.
Inner-city station. Enge gets 3 S-Bahn + IR. Renens is a similar inner satellite but gets much higher frequency due to being a junction.
First suburban station. Only 2 S-Bahn lines but still 4 trains/hr. City101 analog has similar frequency — both suburbs near the hub.
Quiet lakeside suburb. Same 2 lines = 4/hr floor. La Conversion gets similar frequency — but is 54km from Geneva vs 5km from ZH HB.
Rüschlikon: quiet village 6.5km out still gets 4/hr. Bossière at 56km from Geneva gets only 2/hr — worst in corridor. Even ZH's quiet suburbs beat City101's weakest.
Major junction (Lucerne line branches here). 3 S-Bahn + IR. Morges is City101's mid-corridor junction (Morges-Lausanne Y). Both are transit-rich nodes.

THE key comparison station. Oberrieden is the Zurich network's minimum — only S8, only 2/hr. But that's still higher than St-Saphorin (0.0) and equal to Bossière (2.0). The Zurich minimum EQUALS City101's near-minimum.
Horgen gets S2 (express) + S8 (local) = 4/hr. Nyon similarly gets express + local on City101. But Nyon is a significant town — Horgen is a village getting equivalent service.
4 S-Bahn lines + IR + branch to Einsiedeln. Wädenswil is Zurich's analog to Vevey — lakeside junction where a branch line adds extra frequency. Both are cultural towns.
24.5km from hub, still 4/hr from 2 overlapping lines. La Tour-de-Peilz gets same 4/hr — but at 68km from Geneva. Distance-matched parity despite radically different network topology.

Second single-line station. 27km from hub = 2/hr. Épesses at 62km from Geneva gets 3.0 — actually slightly more. But Épesses has ZERO workspaces and ZERO WiFi. Frequency alone doesn't make continuity.
Terminal junction where multiple lines converge. 5 S-Bahn + IR. Montreux is the eastern pole of City101. Both serve as 'end of the main line' hubs with strong frequency.
